Summary of what was found: Unilever has a recent family on making highly labeled plastic packs more recyclable by tuning label thickness and NIR detectability, directly relevant to cosmetic and personal-care bottles that rely on full-wrap branding.
Detailed patent mapping: Publication family: US20240282222A1; family ID 77801512. Assignee: Conopco Inc. d/b/a Unilever. Priority year: 2021. Filing year: 2022. Publication year: 2024. Jurisdiction: US with WO2023041323A1 and EP4401972A1 family members. Legal status: pending. Component: bottle article plus label system. Material route: recyclable polymer container with polymeric label and high label coverage. Claimed invention: recyclable container plus label covering at least 70% of total area, with label substrate thickness about 40 to less than 60 µm so NIR sorting can identify container. Problem solved: sortability/recyclability despite large-area labels common in beauty/personal care. Cosmetic relevance: shampoo, body wash, skincare, and rigid bottle formats with sleeves/high-coverage labels. Premium vs mass-market fit: strongest mass-market and masstige; also relevant to premium label-heavy packs. Mapped topics: S19, S20. IP risk signal: Moderate patent density / monitor claims; brand-owned recyclability route. White-space note: alternative label constructions, lower coverage, or non-label decoration routes may remain. Caution: not lightweighting by itself; strongest where recycled-content bottles need recyclability-safe decoration.
Source name: A recyclable article for packaging — Google Patents — https://patents.google.com/patent/US20240282222A1/en

Summary of what was found: Unilever has a PCR-container family aimed at making black or dark recycled HDPE packaging NIR-sortable by combining natural-PCR outer layers with high-colored-PCR inner layers and detectable colorant masterbatch.
Detailed patent mapping: Publication family: WO2021151797A1; related EP4096928B1, JP7615154B2, CN115023347B. Assignee: Unilever Global IP Ltd / Unilever IP Holdings BV / Conopco Inc. Priority year: 2020. Filing year: 2021. Publication year: 2021 with post-2022 national publications/grants. Jurisdiction: WO/PCT plus national phases. Legal status: PCT ceased; national family members continue. Component: multilayer rigid packaging article. Material route: multilayer recycled HDPE using N-PCR / J-PCR with NIR-detectable colorant. Claimed invention: sustainable packaging article substantially free of virgin plastic, outer natural-PCR layer, inner layer with at least 50% colored PCR, and NIR-detectable masterbatch. Problem solved: uses darker mixed-color PCR without losing optical acceptability or automated sorting. Cosmetic relevance: high for opaque personal-care bottles where rHDPE aesthetics, odor, and sorting are barriers. Premium vs mass-market fit: best for mass-market personal care; limited premium due to dark PCR aesthetics. Mapped topics: S19, S11, S20. IP risk signal: Dominant assignee concentration / brand-owned route / FTO review recommended for black-PCR bottle strategies. White-space note: clear/light-tint PCR structures, mono-material decorated packs, or non-black PCR architectures may remain. Caution: older-priority family; use as risk marker.
Source name: Post consumer resin packaging — Google Patents — https://patents.google.com/patent/WO2021151797A1/en

Summary of what was found: Albéa’s PCR tube family is one of the clearest recent beauty-packaging filings, placing PCR in multilayer squeeze tubes while preserving printability, outer-surface quality, barrier options, and mechanical performance.
Detailed patent mapping: Publication family: US20230112847A1 / US12441519B2; related EP4166316A1. Assignee: Albéa Services. Priority year: 2021. Filing year: 2022. Publication year: 2023. Grant year: 2025 US. Jurisdiction: US / EP. Legal status: US family active. Component: cosmetic/personal-care flexible tube. Material route: multilayer polyolefin tube with PCR layers, virgin outer layer, optional EVOH barrier. Claimed invention: side wall formed from 200-400 µm multilayer sheet including virgin polyolefin layer and PCR/recycled layer; outermost virgin layer preserves printability and appearance. Problem solved: prior blown-film PCR tubes produced poor surface quality; route keeps PCR while maintaining decoration and perceived quality. Cosmetic relevance: very high for tubes. Premium vs mass-market fit: strong mass-market and dermocosmetics; conditional premium depending on outer gloss and print quality. Mapped topics: S14, S19, S20. IP risk signal: High patent density / supplier-owned route / FTO review recommended. White-space note: mono-material PP tubes, higher-PCR single-material tubes, or cap/shoulder/lightweight combinations may remain. Caution: priority 2021 but direct beauty relevance and 2025 grant.
Source name: PCR flexible tube container — Google Patents — https://patents.google.com/patent/US20230112847A1/en

Summary of what was found: TotalEnergies has a very recent family on food-contact multilayer hollow articles with recycled PE, highly relevant to cosmetic bottles through the logic of high recycled content plus controlled multilayer blow molding.

Legal status: pending application. Component: multilayer hollow articles. Material route: two- or three-layer polyethylene hollow article with at least 30 wt% recycled PE. Claimed invention: process for preparing recycled-containing PE compositions and forming multilayer hollow articles; recycled PE resin selected within defined MI range and article contains at least 30 wt% recycled PE. Problem solved: bottle-grade recycled PE causes blow-molding/process variability; route controls properties for multilayer hollow articles. Cosmetic relevance: high for personal-care bottles; food-contact-grade route is potentially transferable to cosmetics. Premium vs mass-market fit: strongest mass-market and pharmacy/dermocosmetic bottles. Mapped topics: S19, S11, S20. IP risk signal: Recent active filings / higher caution; strong FTO relevance for multilayer rPE bottle routes. White-space note: mono-material or non-food-contact beauty implementations using different layer distributions or outer aesthetics may remain. Caution: cross-industry route, not beauty-specific branding/compatibility patent.

Summary of what was found: LyondellBasell/Equistar has a recent polymer recyclate processes family focused on upgrading LDPE recyclate by visbreaking/devolatilization and compounding, relevant because odor and stable processing are major PCR barriers in beauty packs.

Material route: LDPE recyclate processing by visbreaking and devolatilization followed by compounding with polyolefins. Claimed invention: methods for processing LDPE recyclate to improve processing characteristics and remove waste byproducts. Problem solved: odor, volatiles, and poor processability in recyclate streams. Cosmetic relevance: important for pouch films, tubes, closures, and bottles where odor-sensitive formulations are involved. Premium vs mass-market fit: strongest mass-market; premium conditional due to surface/odor demands. Mapped topics: S19, S11, S13, S20.
